UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the SportSphere platform, capable of being a service provider or a consumer, managing their activities and listings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's unique email address, used for communication.

username.description: A unique username chosen by the user for identification on the platform.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture or avatar.

bio.description: A short biography or description provided by the user, especially for service providers.

location.description: The user's primary geographical location or service area.

isProvider.description: A flag indicating if the user is a service provider offering listings (true) or primarily a consumer (false).

registrationDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the user registered on the SportSphere platform.

Listing.title: Listing
Listing.description: Represents a service, product, or event offered by a provider across various sports categories on the SportSphere marketplace.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Listing entity.

providerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity of the provider who created this listing. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Listing)

title.description: The main title or name of the listing.

description.description: A detailed explanation of the service, product, or event being offered.

sport.description: The specific sport this listing pertains to (e.g., Football, Tennis).

location.description: The geographical location pertinent to the listing, such as a venue address, service area, or meeting point.

price.description: The numerical price for the listing. The unit (e.g., per hour, per session, fixed) should be specified in the description or title.

currency.description: The currency code for the listed price (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP').

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs pointing to images or photos showcasing the listing.

availability.description: Details regarding the availability of the service or product, which could be a descriptive string or structured schedule information.

status.description: The current publication status of the listing (e.g., 'active', 'draft', 'archived', 'suspended').

tags.description: A collection of keywords or tags to enhance searchability and categorization of the listing.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the listing was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the listing was last modified.

BookingRequest.title: BookingRequest
BookingRequest.description: Represents a booking or inquiry request initiated by a consumer for a specific listing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BookingRequest entity.

requesterId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity of the user who made this booking/inquiry request. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N BookingRequest)

providerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity of the provider whose listing is being requested. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N BookingRequest)

listingId.description: Reference to the Listing entity for which this request is made. (Relationship: Listing 1:N BookingRequest)

requestedDateTime.description: The specific date and time that the consumer is requesting for the service or event.

message.description: An optional message from the requester detailing their needs or questions related to the booking.

status.description: The current status of the booking request (e.g., 'pending', 'accepted', 'rejected', 'cancelled', 'completed').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the booking request was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the booking request was last updated (e.g., status changed).

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a direct communication exchanged between two users within the SportSphere platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

senderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity of the message sender. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Message)

receiverId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity of the message receiver. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Message)

listingId.description: Optional reference to a Listing entity if the message pertains to a specific listing. (Relationship: Listing 0:1 Message)

content.description: The actual text content of the message.

sentAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the message was sent.

isRead.description: A boolean flag indicating whether the message has been read by the receiver.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the user whose `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Publicly accessible listings with 'active' status. Read access is public. Write/update/delete access is restricted to the listing's `providerId` (denormalized).

params.name: listingId
params.description: The unique ID of the listing.

definition.description: Private listings (e.g., 'draft', 'archived', 'suspended') managed by a service provider. Access is restricted to the `userId` matching `request.auth.uid`. The document includes `providerId` which must match the `userId` path parameter. Includes denormalized 'providerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the provider, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: listingId
params.description: The unique ID of the private listing.

definition.description: Represents a booking or inquiry request. Accessible by both the `requesterId` and the `providerId` (denormalized in the document). Includes denormalized 'requesterId' and 'providerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: bookingRequestId
params.description: The unique ID of the booking request.

definition.description: Direct communication messages. Accessible by both `senderId` and `receiverId` (denormalized in the document). Includes denormalized 'senderId' and 'receiverId' for authorization independence.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique ID of the message.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for SportSphere is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling, ensuring robust security rules and efficient query capabilities (QAPs).

1.  **UserProfile (/userProfiles/{userId}):** Each user's profile is stored in a top-level collection, with the document ID (`userId`) directly corresponding to the authenticated user's UID (`request.auth.uid`). This path-based ownership ensures that profile data is private and easily secured. Authorization Independence is achieved as rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with `userId` from the path, eliminating the need for `get()` operations. This naturally supports QAPs for listing the user's own profile.

2.  **Listing Segregation:** Listings are split into two structurally segregated collections based on their security posture and publication status:
    *   **Public Listings (/listings/{listingId}):** This collection holds 'active' listings that are publicly discoverable. Read access is public, adhering to QAPs for broad search and discovery without filtering rules. Write, update, and delete operations are restricted to the `providerId` field (denormalized within each listing document), ensuring Authorization Independence for provider actions.
    *   **Provider's Private Listings (/userProfiles/{userId}/providerListings/{listingId}):** This subcollection is for listings in 'draft', 'archived', or 'suspended' states. It is nested under the `userProfiles` collection (`userId`) to enforce direct ownership. The `providerId` within the document is denormalized and MUST match the `userId` in the path, providing a redundant check and maintaining Authorization Independence without `get()` calls. This structure supports QAPs by allowing providers to list only their own private listings via the path-based authorization.

3.  **BookingRequest (/bookingRequests/{bookingRequestId}):** Booking requests are collaborative entities involving both a `requesterId` and a `providerId`. By storing these IDs directly within each `BookingRequest` document, Authorization Independence is maintained. Rules can check if `request.auth.uid` matches either `requesterId` or `providerId` directly from the `resource.data`. This top-level collection supports QAPs through direct queries using `where('requesterId', '==', request.auth.uid)` or `where('providerId', '==', request.auth.uid)`, allowing users to efficiently retrieve all relevant booking requests without unauthorized data exposure.

4.  **Message (/messages/{messageId}):** Direct communication messages are stored in a top-level collection. Each `Message` document explicitly includes `senderId` and `receiverId`. This denormalization ensures Authorization Independence, allowing rules to evaluate access by checking if `request.auth.uid` matches either the `senderId` or `receiverId` in the `resource.data`. For QAPs, users can retrieve their messages by querying `where('senderId', '==', request.auth.uid)` or `where('receiverId', '==', request.auth.uid)`, enabling efficient fetching of all communications relevant to the authenticated user.
